# Post-M18 Analysis 3: Channel-Overlap Matrix
## Method
For each model, I computed top-1% channels from prompt-averaged activation magnitudes per layer and decode position, then reported adjacent-position Jaccard overlaps averaged across layers. Granite-Small has an exact 500-position-grid analysis from the Phase 9 M11 activation packet; the other existing model packets only contain the coarser preregistered grid.
## Exact Granite-Small 500-position grid
### Granite-Small M11 fine grid
Source: `experimental/outlier_migrate/phase9/results/om_phase9_m11_granite_small_vac12_20260516T010728Z`. Positions: `[100, 500, 1000, 1500, 2000, 2500, 3000, 3500, 4000, 4500, 5000, 5500, 6000, 6500, 7000, 7500, 8000, 8500, 9000, 9500, 10000]`.

| Adjacent positions | Mean Jaccard | Median Jaccard | Min layer Jaccard | Max layer Jaccard |

## Interpretation
Granite-Small fine-grid overlap changes smoothly rather than showing a single obvious cliff, although wider position gaps naturally have lower overlap. The non-Granite packets do not have every-500-position evidence, so cross-model statements should be limited to their measured grids. This supports gradual set drift more than a single abrupt transition, but the evidence is activation-only.
